commands.repair_reason: The remaining action is maintainer API/compatibility approval; there is no narrow automated repair because the review found no blocking implementation defect.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the diff adds no dependencies, workflows, permissions, or secret handling, and documents that the new stream is not integrity-attested. (sha=ddbc5c7ce463ac829c3847db754a3d0fc3ee3f0b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `onModelDiagnosticEvent` to the plugin SDK diagnostic-runtime and compat surfaces so plugins can subscribe to an allowlisted trusted `model.*` diagnostic stream.

PR surface: Source +64, Tests +143, Docs +1. Total +208 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main drops trusted events from `onDiagnosticEvent`, while the model lifecycle events are trusted diagnostics. I did not run a live repro in this read-only review, but the code path and PR proof make the gap high-confidence.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is maintainer API/compatibility approval; there is no narrow automated repair because the review found no blocking implementation defect.

target.title: feat(plugin-sdk): onModelDiagnosticEvent for trusted model.* events
target.body:
## Why

Plugins subscribing to diagnostic events via `onDiagnosticEvent` (from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/diagnostic-runtime`) never see the `model.*` lifecycle. The public API filter at [`src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts:879-886`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts#L879-L886):

```ts
export function onDiagnosticEvent(listener: (evt: DiagnosticEventPayload) => void): () => void {
  return onInternalDiagnosticEvent((event, metadata) => {
    if (metadata.trusted || event.type === "log.record") {
      return;
    }
    listener(event);
  });
}
```

drops **every** event whose dispatcher metadata is `trusted: true`. The runtime emits `model.call.started`, `model.call.completed`, `model.call.error`, `model.usage`, and `model.failover` via `emitTrustedDiagnosticEvent`. So the entire model lifecycle is invisible to plugins.

This breaks observability/cost-attribution plugins. A telemetry bridge plugin needs:

- `model.call.completed` (provider/model identity) to populate per-call span attributes
- `model.usage` (token counts, cost) for per-call attribution

Today both subscriptions are dead code against the trusted emit paths. Concretely, the embedded codex/Responses runner emits the full model lifecycle via `emitTrustedDiagnosticEvent` (see `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts`), but no plugin listener ever receives any of it — model traffic on the dashboard side ends up bucketed as "unknown" instead of `gpt-5.5`/etc.

## Approach

Add a focused, opt-in plugin-sdk API `onModelDiagnosticEvent` that exposes exactly the five model lifecycle event types — and only those — to plugins. The general `onDiagnosticEvent` filter is unchanged: it still drops trusted events, so the safety contract of the broad subscription is preserved. Plugins that want model telemetry have to opt in explicitly.

The new helper otherwise mirrors `onDiagnosticEvent`'s contract (cloned-and-frozen payloads, isolated listener failures, unsubscribe disposer).

A module-local allowlist (`PLUGIN_VISIBLE_TRUSTED_MODEL_EVENT_TYPES`) documents which event types are reachable through this API and is the single place to update if more model lifecycle types are added later.

## Files

- `src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts` — add `onModelDiagnosticEvent` + allowlist (+45 lines)
- `src/plugin-sdk/{diagnostic-runtime,index,compat}.ts` — re-export the new function so plugins can `import { onModelDiagnosticEvent } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/diagnostic-runtime"` (or the top-level barrel / compat subpath)
- `src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts` — three new tests:
  1. delivers all five `model.*` trusted types and filters non-model traffic
  2. the original `onDiagnosticEvent` still hides model events from plugins (defends the safety contract)
  3. unsubscribe disposer stops further delivery
- `CHANGELOG.md` — Unreleased / Changes entry

Total: 6 files, +158 / −2.

## Tests

- `src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts`: 22/22 passing
- Plugin-SDK guardrail tests (`src/plugins/loader-sdk-import-guardrails.test.ts`, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-root-alias.test.ts`): 44 tests, all passing

## End-to-end validation

Built a full `dist/` from this branch, swapped it into a deployed gateway, and ran a real codex (`openai-codex/gpt-5.5`) turn from a Telegram-driven session. Bridge plugin logs show **both** subscriptions on startup:

```
[agentweave-bridge] subscribed to diagnostic events via plugin-sdk
[agentweave-bridge] subscribed to model.* trusted events via plugin-sdk
```

…and during the turn the bridge now receives the `model.usage` event for codex/gpt-5.5 (previously dead-code path):

```
[agentweave-bridge] event: model.usage sessionKey: agent:main:main source: undefined
```

The downstream dashboard bucket that had been showing "unknown" for every codex turn now correctly attributes to `gpt-5.5`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Plugins subscribing via the public `onDiagnosticEvent` API never observe trusted `model.*` lifecycle events (`model.call.started/completed/error`, `model.usage`, `model.failover`) because the existing filter at `src/infra/diagnostic-events.ts:879` drops every event whose dispatcher metadata is `trusted: true`. On my deployed install, this surfaced as Codex `gpt-5.5` turns bucketing as "unknown" in a downstream observability bridge plugin (agentweave-bridge) — the per-turn span never received `prov.llm.{provider,model}` because the bridge's `model.usage` / `model.call.completed` handlers were dead code against the trusted emit paths.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw v2026.5.10-beta.1 gateway running under `systemctl --user` on Linux (Debian 12, Node 24.13 via nvm) on my NAS (192.168.1.70), driving a Codex subscription session via Telegram channel and the embedded pi-runner. Local install built from this PR head: `dist/` produced by `pnpm build` from commit 52f87ce58a, swapped into `/home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist/`, gateway restarted. Bridge plugin installed under `~/.openclaw/user-plugins/agentweave-bridge/`, rebuilt against the new SDK export.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```
  # 1. Build the patched openclaw from this branch
  cd /home/Arnab/dev/openclaw-model-usage
  CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
  CI=true pnpm build

  # 2. Install over the canonical openclaw dist/
  systemctl --user stop openclaw-gateway
  mv /home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist /home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist.bak
  cp -r dist /home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist
  systemctl --user start openclaw-gateway

  # 3. Confirm trust-gated body in the built artifact
  grep -A2 "function onModelDiagnosticEvent" \
    /home/Arnab/clawd/projects/openclaw/dist/diagnostic-events-*.js

  # 4. Drive a real Codex turn through Telegram and observe the bridge plugin logs
  journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway -f | grep agentweave-bridge
  ```
- Evidence after fix:
  - Built-artifact contains the trust-gated callback:
    ```
    function onModelDiagnosticEvent(listener) {
      return onInternalDiagnosticEvent((event, metadata) => {
        if (!metadata.trusted) return;
    ```
  - Gateway startup logs from `journalctl --user -u openclaw-gateway` show the plugin subscribing to BOTH streams (previously only one):
    ```
    [agentweave-bridge] OTel SDK started, exporting to: http://192.168.1.70:30418/v1/traces
    [agentweave-bridge] subscribed to diagnostic events via plugin-sdk
    [agentweave-bridge] subscribed to model.* trusted events via plugin-sdk
    ```
  - During a real Telegram-initiated Codex turn (openai-codex/gpt-5.5), the bridge plugin now receives a trusted `model.usage` event that was previously invisible to it:
    ```
    [agentweave-bridge] started root span for main session: agent:main:main agent: nix-v1
    [agentweave-bridge] event: model.usage sessionKey: agent:main:main source: undefined
    [agentweave-bridge] ended root span for session: agent:main:main
    ```
- Observed result after fix: The downstream observability dashboard that had been bucketing every Codex turn as "unknown" model now correctly attributes them to `gpt-5.5`, with cost figures flowing through the bridge's pricing fallback. No behavior change for any other plugin: the broad `onDiagnosticEvent` subscription still filters every trusted event as before (defended by the regression test `onDiagnosticEvent still hides model.* trusted events from plugins`). A separate regression test confirms forged untrusted `model.*` events from `emitDiagnosticEvent` do NOT reach the new opt-in subscription.
- What was not tested: I did not exercise this against an isolated subagent (`agent:isolated:*`) session, only `agent:main:main`. Did not run on macOS or Windows; tested under Linux Node 24.13 only. Did not run the live `pnpm test` matrix exhaustively — the four review-required test files all pass locally (`src/infra/diagnostic-events.test.ts` 23/23, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts` 5/5, `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-root-alias.test.ts` 13/13, `src/plugins/loader-sdk-import-guardrails.test.ts` 8/8) plus `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` and `pnpm build` are clean.
target.author: arniesaha
